address: 209/2 Sridom Chai Road, Mae Rim District

description.en:
## Overview

Elephant Nature Park is Thailand's most respected ethical elephant sanctuary, located in the Mae Taeng valley about 60 kilometers north of Chiang Mai. Founded by the renowned conservationist Sangduen "Lek" Chailert, the park serves as a rescue and rehabilitation center for elephants that have been abused, injured, or retired from the logging and tourism industries. It is a no-riding facility where visitors observe and walk alongside elephants in a natural riverside setting.

## History

Lek Chailert established Elephant Nature Park in the 1990s after witnessing the widespread mistreatment of captive elephants across Thailand. What began as a small rescue effort has grown into a 250-acre sanctuary that is now home to dozens of elephants, along with rescued dogs, cats, and water buffalo. The park has become a model for ethical wildlife tourism in Southeast Asia and has inspired similar sanctuaries throughout the region.

## What to See

Visitors can choose from half-day or full-day programs. Half-day morning visits begin with hotel pickup around 7:30 AM and include guided walks alongside the elephant herd, time to watch the elephants bathe and play in the river, and a vegan buffet lunch featuring Asian and international dishes. Full-day visits offer a more immersive experience with additional time to learn about individual elephants and their rescue stories. The park's landscape of open meadows, forested hills, and the Mae Taeng River provides a peaceful backdrop that feels worlds away from the city.

## Practical Info

Half-day morning programs cost 2,500 THB per person and full-day visits cost 3,500 THB, both with a 50 percent discount for children up to age 11. Prices include round-trip transportation from Chiang Mai, entrance fees, guides, and lunch. Booking in advance is strongly recommended as the park limits daily visitors to minimize stress on the animals. The park operates year-round, but the cool season from November to February offers the most comfortable weather. Wear comfortable shoes and bring sunscreen, a hat, and a camera.

entry_fee: 2,500 THB (half-day), 3,500 THB (full-day), 50% off for children under 12
visit_duration: Half day (4-5 hours) or full day (8-9 hours)
best_time_to_visit: November to February for comfortable weather; book well in advance
tips: Book online at least a few days ahead as spots fill up quickly
This is a no-riding sanctuary so expect walking and observing only
Wear comfortable shoes and bring sunscreen as much of the visit is outdoors
